themeWords.meaning: Ephraim; a son of Joseph and the dominant northern tribe, symbol of the Northern Kingdom
a pact, treaty, alliance, or agreement, often between God and His people

sentenceTheme: The LORD indicts Ephraim and Judah for deceit and foreign reliance, recalls Jacob’s striving, calls them to return with mercy and justice, and warns of judgment.

verses.text: Ephraim feedeth on wind, and followeth after the east wind: he daily increaseth lies and desolation; and they do make a covenant with the Assyrians, and oil is carried into Egypt.

highlightedWords.meaning: Ephraim; a son of Joseph and the dominant northern tribe, symbol of the Northern Kingdom
a pact, treaty, alliance, or agreement

verses.text: The LORD hath also a controversy with Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways; according to his doings will he recompense him.

highlightedWords.meaning: a dispute, legal case, or contention

verses.text: He took his brother by the heel in the womb, and by his strength he had power with God:

highlightedWords.meaning: heel; figuratively, one who supplants or trips up

verses.text: Yea, he had power over the angel, and prevailed: he wept, and made supplication unto him: he found him in Bethel, and there he spake with us;

highlightedWords.meaning: House of God; a place in Ephraim

verses.text: Even the LORD God of hosts; the LORD is his memorial.

highlightedWords.meaning: armies, hosts; heavenly or earthly forces

verses.text: Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.
He is a merchant, the balances of deceit are in his hand: he loveth to oppress.
And Ephraim said, Yet I am become rich, I have found me out substance: in all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that were sin.
And I that am the LORD thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.
I have also spoken by the prophets, and I have multiplied visions, and used similitudes, by the ministry of the prophets.
Is there iniquity in Gilead? surely they are vanity: they sacrifice bullocks in Gilgal; yea, their altars are as heaps in the furrows of the fields.

highlightedWords.meaning: Gilead; a region east of the Jordan

verses.text: And Jacob fled into the country of Syria, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept sheep.
And by a prophet the LORD brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet was he preserved.
Ephraim provoked him to anger most bitterly: therefore shall he leave his blood upon him, and his reproach shall his Lord return unto him.
